positionData.text: Hi everyone!!  I had an amazing year representing you and your fellow students and it would be a privilege to do it again. We started so many projects this year and I want nothing less than to see them grow and finish. As a political science student being an advocate is something I want to do for the rest of my life, and it was so much fun being able to do it this year. let’s work together and make this campus a better place for everybody.

My decision to run for Vice-Chair comes from my genuine commitment to supporting the Board, strengthening the U-SU, and advocating for students here at Cal State LA. I firmly believe in collaborative engagement and how it can indeed elevate student life, and I hope to contribute to this community further by uplifting both my fellow directors and the campus as a whole. Through my leadership positions in the Delta Phi Epsilon sorority, new role as Director of Events for the Marketing Club, involvement in the ROOTS Scholars program, and background in various external experiences, I have consistently worked to build spaces where creativity and connection are transformed into impactful initiatives. These opportunities have solidified my skills in organizing effectively, communicating across teams, and supporting the work of others – all of which I will bring to the Board. More importantly, my approach is centered on leadership with heart, which I believe to be my strongest suit: leading with empathy, purpose, and sincerity. As your Vice-Chair, I would be a dependable source of support to the Board and to you, helping ensure that the U-SU remains a space where everyone truly feels heard, valued, and empowered to belong.

As a third-year student at Cal State LA, I am excited about the opportunity to potentially serve on the U-SU Board of Directors for a second year and to continue building on the work started this school year, in collaboration with fellow board members, on initiatives that support and engage the student community. Over the past year, my consistent involvement with VITA has grown into a supervisory role, and in the coming weeks I will begin serving as a Summer Orientation Leader, experiences that will hone my leadership, reliability, and empowerment skills to support students throughout their college journey. These opportunities have reinforced my dedication to fostering a welcoming campus environment where students feel encouraged to get involved, discover communities and clubs, build meaningful connections, and develop their own path toward growth and success.

I am currently a junior at Cal State LA majoring in Computer Science and minoring in Biomedical Engineering, Psychology, and Math. I am interested in AI and Machine Learning as well as their applications to benefit the world. I enjoy taking on challenges, learning new things, and I am passionate about policy development and supporting student success. I have been serving as a Student Director for the past two years, and I am running for the same position again to continue contributing to our campus community. My involvement with the U-SU Board of Directors as a Student Director and Fiscal Committee Chair, as well as with ASI as an Undergraduate Academic Senator, has given me extensive leadership experience at Cal State LA. Through these roles, I have worked to address student concerns, improve academic policies, and support initiatives that enhance our learning environment. For example, as Fiscal Committee Chair, I have worked to present our budget situation to the board and contribute to decisions on the path we should take. With strong leadership experience both within and beyond Cal State LA and interpersonal skills, I aim to connect with and advocate for students, engage the community, and contribute to positive campus change providing guidance for success. Let’s continue working together for a better future at Cal State LA!

Hi, my name is Angel Bernal. I am a third-year History Major with a Double Minor in Art and Chicano Studies. The reason I have chosen to put my hat in the ring for this position is that I want to be part of the puzzle to help students. I want to see and help students so they can be students while also having a life outside of school. And I know that it may take a lot of time and work. I hope you let me start this project.

My name is Ariana Rodriguez, and I am a second-year psychology student. I am interested in running for student director because I want to be an advocate for Cal State LA students. I am committed to helping build an environment here on campus of academic opportunity and service, and to be part of the change alongside with other Cal State LA members.

As a proud senior at Cal State LA, I am excited to run for the USU Board of Directors and continue building my community at this institution that I cherish so much. My lived experiences as a first-generation Latina in and out of college, combined with my ability to foster relationships with people of all backgrounds, make me an ideal candidate for this role. As a student director, I hope to represent our university and sharpen my professional skills while I complete the criminal justice program, alongside working in retail and serving as an intern at a nonprofit organization centered around transformative justice.

Hello everyone! My name is Ina and I transferred to Cal State LA last semester. As a transfer student and after almost a year of being here on campus, I have the ideal perspective of what current and future students want and need. I know what aspects I’d like to improve on for the student body, and I want to be more involved on campus and create an impact for Golden Eagles. I’m a very creative person and possess great organizational and team work skills from my past involvement in clubs. On top of that, I’m highly motivated to create change on campus, especially highlighting the relevance of students’ voices. For example, I would like to help ensure that there is more accessibility for student facilities and visibility for students in services, such as better way finding and more events. The USU is already a hub where students can connect but I believe it can become greater than it is and I wanna be a part of that growth. I also want students to know they matter because the USU is a bridge between students and the community, and both are valued. As a student director I’m ready to lead and want to bring my and your ideas to life on making Cal State LA go from a “commuter-ty” to a community

I am qualified to be part of the U-SU Board of Directors because of the learning experiences I have gained at and outside of Cal State LA. In programs such as Social Justice Club at my community college, where I was the social media manager posting our events to the student body for my sophomore year. Although this is my first year as a transfer at Cal State LA, I offer a unique perspective as a disabilities student who aims to encourage all student to embody their full potential. I am a member of the Pre Law Society as well as the Political Science Association. I have much to learn and I would be honored to be considered for a Board position of the Directors of U-SU.

I am a strong candidate for the U-SU Board of  Directors due to my incredible leadership skills that I used at my previous place of employment. As an acting shift lead I would delegate duties and responsibilities to coworkers regularly. I have awesome organizational abilities that supersede all the other candidates. I have been an outstanding student this year at CSULA. I am on the NSS Dean’s list. I was Interim President for Psi Chi the International Club on Psychology and helped transition the club into the new year at CSULA. I look forward to this next year coming as it will be my senior year. I will continue my educational career here at CSULA. Thank you for allowing me to run for this position!
